summary: Frontend-only verification of the TOTAL admin portal redesign (dark slate-950 sidebar, emerald accents, collapsible sidebar with persistence, emerald page-header accent bar, redesigned Dashboard hero, mobile drawer, parent portal regression). All 23 sub-checks PASSED across desktop and mobile viewports.

verified_checks.sidebar_group_headers: PASS (Academics, Finance, People, System all rendered)
verified_checks.page_h1_emerald_accent_bar: PASS on /students, /classes, /fees, /attendance (::before width 5px, linear-gradient emerald-500 -> emerald-600, padding-left 18px)
verified_checks.active_nav_emerald_left_accent_bar: PASS (gradient bg + left absolute span present)
verified_checks.parent_portal_emerald_gradient_intact: PASS (login screen + emerald-to-teal classes still present)

critical_code_review_comments: Layout.js is ~310 lines, well within the 700-line guideline; clean separation of NavContent/NavLinkItem.

index.css rule 'main h1.tracking-tight::before' is a broad selector — relies on every admin page H1 carrying class 'tracking-tight'. Confirmed working on 4 sampled pages; if a new page omits 'tracking-tight' it will silently lose the accent. Consider a dedicated PageHeader component (file exists but unused) for stronger guarantee.
Sidebar auto-collapse logic in useEffect only collapses (never auto-expands) when window>=1100; this matches the requested 3c behaviour (manual toggle wins after first interaction).
Dashboard greeting is hard-coded to 'Admin' regardless of role/name — minor copy consideration only, not a bug per the spec which says 'Good Morning/Afternoon/Evening, Admin'.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Admin portal redesign verified end-to-end. localStorage key 'sidebar-collapsed' should be cleared at the start of any test that asserts default-expanded behaviour (page.evaluate(() => localStorage.removeItem('sidebar-collapsed'))). Parent portal not touched in this round and still renders the emerald gradient login screen.
